methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $40M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Fourth Power is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Asegun Henry and Arvin Ganesan, having raised $40M+ to date. The broader Climate & Energy sector includes nine private peers (Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, Electric Hydrogen among them) and one public comparable (Linamar, ticker LNZA), though none of these peers occupy the same stage and valuation tier simultaneously.

The stage-progression method is the primary applicable triangulation approach. Using the $40M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier for progression from Series A to subsequent rounds yields an implied valuation of $80M. This method is standard for early-stage capital formation but relies on the assumption that deployment efficiency and risk factors remain consistent with sector norms. A peer-median analysis proved inapplicable; while nine same-sector peers exist, zero share Fourth Power's specific stage and valuation tier, making a median-based cross-check unreliable and potentially misleading.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $68M to $92M, centered on $80M. This range reflects ±15% bounds around the stage-progression method output. Key uncertainties include the absence of narrow-set peer validation and reliance on a single triangulation method. Any valuation within this band should be treated as provisional pending either emergence of closer-stage comparable data or access to Fourth Power's current unit economics and burn metrics.
